items.a: Garden formal. Long dresses, agbada, gele, and a well cut suit. The house colours are ivory, emerald, and gold, so lean that way if you can. The ceremony is on grass, so kind shoes are wise.
Yes. Little ones are welcome to play on the lawn, and we have set aside a quiet corner for tired feet.
The day begins at 10 in the morning. Please aim to be seated in the garden by a quarter to ten so we can start on time.
The whole day takes place in the garden at Redemption Camp, Mowe, Ogun State. There is a map and a maps link on the Venue page.
Yes. There is ample parking within Redemption Camp, with stewards on hand to guide you to the garden from 9 in the morning.

items.q: Can I take photographs during the ceremony?
items.a: We kindly ask that phones and cameras stay away during the ceremony so everyone can be fully present. A full gallery will be shared with all guests afterwards.
Your presence is gift enough. For those who have asked, please see our Blessings page, where small contributions go toward our first home.
Lunch in the garden offers standard, vegetarian, and halal options. Do let our chief bridesmaid know of any allergies in advance.

items.q: How do I get in touch?
items.a: Our chief bridesmaid Folasade can be reached at folasade@etomotosho.com for all matters of the day.
